text:
Handling Deadlines Effectively

Meeting deadlines is a key skill in both academic and professional life. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller tasks and set realistic milestones. Prioritization is essential—focus on the most important or urgent tasks first, rather than getting lost in minor details. Scope management means knowing when to say "no" to extra requests that could derail your progress. Finally, communication is crucial: if you anticipate a delay, inform your team or supervisor early, so they can adjust expectations or offer support. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and deliver quality work on time.
